mitigations.mitigation_name: Remove or disable the attacker’s local tooling on the Ubuntu host, especially command-line utilities being used for file discovery, staging, archiving, and upload attempts

args.command: which find tar curl && chmod -x /usr/bin/find /usr/bin/tar /usr/bin/curl && ls -l /usr/bin/find /usr/bin/tar /usr/bin/curl
find /usr/bin -maxdepth 1 \( -name find -o -name tar -o -name curl \) -exec chmod -x {} \; && ls -l /usr/bin/find /usr/bin/tar /usr/bin/curl

mv /usr/bin/find /usr/bin/find.disabled && mv /usr/bin/tar /usr/bin/tar.disabled && mv /usr/bin/curl /usr/bin/curl.disabled && cat >/usr/local/bin/find <<'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
echo 'find disabled by mitigation' >&2
exit 126
EOF
cat >/usr/local/bin/tar <<'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
echo 'tar disabled by mitigation' >&2
exit 126
EOF
cat >/usr/local/bin/curl <<'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
echo 'curl disabled by mitigation' >&2
exit 126
EOF
chmod 755 /usr/local/bin/find /usr/local/bin/tar /usr/local/bin/curl && which find tar curl && ls -l /usr/local/bin/find /usr/local/bin/tar /usr/local/bin/curl /usr/bin/find.disabled /usr/bin/tar.disabled /usr/bin/curl.disabled
